title: gmelina magnifica
intro: Gmelina magnifica, also known as the white teak tree, is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Gmelina magnifica include relief from fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
